title: E-Habitat
summary: A distributed systems dashboard for monitoring temperature, humidity, airflow, and anomaly events across virtual server-room nodes.
description: A monitoring and simulation interface for a distributed server-room environment with telemetry, control inputs, and anomaly injection.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: E-Habitat is an interactive simulation platform that models server-room conditions across multiple virtual nodes. The interface presents telemetry, anomaly states, and controls in a manner that is readable, responsive, and useful for demonstrating distributed monitoring concepts.

sections.title: Technologies
items: Next.js - Handles routing and page structure
React - Building reusable UI components and live dashboard viewing
TypeScript - Safer telemetry and state handling, cleaner component props
FastAPI - Backend endpoints and simulation control APIs
WebSockets - Realtime telemetry updates without constant polling

items.title: Goal
items.content: Present changing environmental conditions across multiple virtual nodes in a readable, streamlined manner.

items.title: Role
items.content: Frontend development, UI design, FastAPI/WebSocket integration

items.title: Features
items.content: Anomaly injection, manual controls, anomaly history, individual user profiles, real-time monitoring, detailed telemetry

items.title: Result
items.content: All core features implemented; effectively demonstrates centralized vs edge-based monitoring.

title: Network Security Lab
summary: Lab focused on building, testing, and securing a connection between client and server using SSL.
description: Secure network connections between a client and server using an SSL client and SSL (single-threaded) server with variable encryption configurations.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The lab involved building a connection between a client and server using network programming, then securing that connection with different encryption algorithms. Wireshark was used to capture and analyze the network traffic.

sections.title: Technologies
items: Python - Used to run and test the client-server connection
Wireshark - Used to capture and analyze packets
OpenSSL - Generated certificates and keys to support TLS configurations
Linux - Working environment for the project

items.title: Goal
items.content: Write client and server code using socket network programming, securing that connection using SSL, testing at least two different encryption configurations

items.title: Role
items.content: End-to-end development. Solo implementation of network connection, encryption configurations, and analysis of packet capture.

items.title: Features
items.content: Client-server connection secured with RSA/AES, RSA/ChaCha20 encryption configurations

items.title: Result
items.content: Successfully protected the confidentiality and integrity of data-in-transit. Data was successfully decrypted with no problems.

title: Portfolio Website
summary: A personal portfolio site focused on clear communication, academic work, and project visualization.
description: A portfolio website that present my relevant work experience, education, and skills in a streamlined, professional manner.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: A portfolio website designed with information accessibility and simplicity in mind. Showcases resume information using a maintainable component-based architecture.

sections.title: Key Libraries
items: React Hook Form - Manages contact form state and submission handling
Zod - Contact form validation to enforce safe data handling
Framer Motion - Adds animations for visual flare
React Icons - Used for consistent iconography
MongoDB - Database to store contact form submissions

items.title: Goal
items.content: Create a personal portfolio for work history and experience. Meet all project requirements as directed.

items.title: Role
items.content: End-to-end development. Frontend with database integration.

items.title: Features
items.content: Responsive design, dynamic routing, JSON data-driven, contact form supported by database, resume integration

items.title: Result
items.content: All project requirements met. Intentionally basic and conservative design for the sake of simplicity.

title: CS Marketplace
summary: An all-inclusive community hub for those in the computer science field. Users are able to login, plan events, and chat in real-time.
description: CS Marketplace is a website that facilitates a centralized location for users to plan events and chat with others, all supported by a fully-featured login system with defined user roles.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: CS Marketplace is a community hub that allows users to create events on an interactive calendar, chat with other users, and login to view tracked event data. Admin users have the ability to restrict users, modify event data, and edit sensitive information. A server-hosted database stores events data and user login information.

sections.title: Key Technologies
items: JavaScript - Handles client-facing interface and backend logic
HTML/CSS - Build the structure and presentation of web pages
Express.js - Manages routing, server-side logic, application flow
bcrypt - Hashes passwords using industry-standard encryption for secure password storage
MySQL - Used for database to store key events and user login information
Google Cloud Platform - Hosted VM used to support MySQL server
Firebase - Used to support chat functionality via widgets

items.title: Goal
items.content: Build a full-featured web application that allows users to login, organize events, chat with others, and view information on an interactive calendar.

items.title: Role
items.content: Full stack development. Built events page and adding/deleting/modifying events functionality, assisted in database construction

items.title: Features
items.content: Events planning, interactive calendar, user login and authentication, defined user-roles (admin, group admin, user)

items.title: Result
items.content: All core project features implemented, user login with persistent sessions and secure password hashing
